reading_LCDM.model: LCDM (w=-1 held, as TFPT demands); only the nu axis can pull

reading_w0waCDM.model: w0waCDM (bound relaxes to 0.163 eV; eff-mass posterior peaks at 0)

axis_A_mnu_w_pincer.caveat: Sigma m_nu bounds are MODEL-DEPENDENT: in LCDM the TFPT floor is in ~3-sigma tension (via the effective-mass posterior; it still sits inside the standard 95% bound 0.0642 eV but outside the Feldman-Cousins 0.053 eV); in w0waCDM the floor is fully compatible (+0.7 sigma) but then w=-1 itself is disfavoured at up to ~4.4 sigma. The pincer: no published model reading lets BOTH TFPT fixed points sit below ~3 sigma simultaneously. Gaussian approximations of published posteriors throughout; SN samples overlap (never stacked).
axis_A_mnu_w_pincer.kill_rule: joint (w=-1, 0.0588 eV) excluded at >= 5 sigma in a single systematics-controlled combination under BOTH model readings

axis_B_alpha_lambda_lock.lock: rho_Lambda/Mbar^4 = (3/4pi^2) exp(-2 alpha^-1)  =>  d ln rho_Lambda/dt = 2 alpha^-1 * (alpha_dot/alpha)

clock_limit_per_yr.source: PTB Yb+ E3/E2 optical clock comparison, Filzinger et al., Phys. Rev. Lett. 130, 253001 (2023), arXiv:2301.03433; data May 2016 - Nov 2022; still the strictest published limit as of 2026-07-02

mutual_exclusion.statement: IF the DESI (w0,wa) preference is real dark-energy dynamics AND the TFPT alpha-Lambda lock holds, the implied alpha drift violates the optical-clock bound by ~10^5.7 -- so 'TFPT + real w(z) evolution' is internally killed; TFPT is only consistent with w = -1 (axis A) AND alpha static (this axis) JOINTLY.

axis_B_alpha_lambda_lock.today: clocks see alpha_dot/alpha = 1.8(2.5)e-19/yr (consistent with zero) -- exactly what TFPT's static fixed point demands; the lock then bounds |d ln Lambda/dt| < ~1.4e-16/yr (2 sigma)
axis_B_alpha_lambda_lock.kill_rule: any confirmed alpha_dot/alpha != 0, or any confirmed Lambda drift violating d ln rho_Lambda/dt = 2 alpha^-1 d(alpha)/alpha/dt

kill_window.note: a future tau-mass at today's Belle II central (1777.09) with sigma <~ 0.024 MeV would be a 5-sigma kill; at today's PDG central (1776.93) sigma <~ 0.008 MeV would be needed. Belle II full dataset aims at O(0.01-0.05) MeV -- the window is live.

tfpt_fixed_point.statement: N_fam = 3 is an exact compiler output (D5+A3+mu4 => E8): there is no spare E8 slot for a 4th light family/sterile state, so TFPT PREDICTS the dissolution of every short-baseline anomaly -- not merely survives it.

fronts.status_note: excludes the single light sterile (3+1) interpretation of the LSND and MiniBooNE anomalies at 95% CL; rules out a significant portion of the gallium/BEST parameter space. First use of two accelerator beams (BNB 0.57% intrinsic nu_e, NuMI 4.6%) to break the nu_e appearance/disappearance degeneracy.

fronts.reference: SAGE/GALLEX calibrations + BEST 2022 (R_in = 0.79 +- 0.05, R_out = 0.77 +- 0.05); combined significance >~ 4-5 sigma
fronts.status_note: the ~20% counting deficit itself PERSISTS unexplained; BEST sees no distance dependence (no oscillation signature), and the large mixing a sterile explanation needs is disfavoured by solar/reactor data; BEST-2 (58Co source) proposed.

direct LSND test at J-PARC MLF: 2 events observed vs 2.3 +- 0.4 expected background (LSND anomaly would add 1.1 +- 0.5) -- no excess, not yet conclusive (~7% of approved POT analysed; ~half of planned POT accumulated by end 2025). JSNS2-II near+far detectors taking data since Nov 2025 (far detector commissioning).

fronts.reference: Fermilab SBN: ICARUS first nu_mu-disappearance results (2022-23 data, Apr 2026); SBND running
fronts.status_note: ICARUS sees no evidence of nu_mu disappearance and sets 90% CL limits on the 3+1 model; the joint SBND+ICARUS two-detector analysis is designed to decide the short-baseline anomalies this decade.

axis_D_sterile_dissolution.today: MicroBooNE's two-beam analysis (Nature, Dec 2025) excludes the single light sterile interpretation of LSND/MiniBooNE at 95% CL and cuts into the gallium/BEST parameter space; the gallium counting deficit itself persists unexplained (no oscillation signature); JSNS2 is running (first data consistent with background); SBND+ICARUS will decide this decade.
axis_D_sterile_dissolution.kill_rule: any confirmed sterile oscillation signal at >= 5 sigma (systematics-controlled; e.g. joint SBND+ICARUS or JSNS2-II)

tfpt_fixed_point.statement: lambda_C = sqrt(phi0(1-phi0)) exactly (frozen v84) + exact CKM unitarity: the first-row deficit MUST dissolve, and TFPT says where -- V_us converges to 0.22438 (between the two kaon routes) and the V_ud side resolves against superallowed (nuclear structure/RC), whose routes the neutron already contradicts.

neutron_lifetime.reading: TFPT's exact V_ud lands the parameter-free tau_n on the BOTTLE side of the beam-bottle puzzle (<1 sigma from UCNtau) and puts the proton-counting beam average at ~ -5 sigma => the beam carries a systematic (proton counting), exactly as the electron-counting J-PARC beam result already suggests. The dark-decay exit (n -> chi) is FORBIDDEN by the same E8 counting as axis D -- TFPT cannot absorb a real beam-bottle split.

axis_E_cabibbo_neutron_lifetime.deciders: PIONEER (pi_e3), lattice f+(0)+fK/fpi, superallowed NS/RC re-evaluations, NIST BL2/BL3, J-PARC full dataset, tau_n/gA consensus
axis_E_cabibbo_neutron_lifetime.kill_rule: converged all-route V_us (S~1) with |V_us - 0.224376| >= 5 sigma; OR a systematics-converged first-row deficit >= 5 sigma; OR a confirmed beam-bottle split established as real new physics (dark decay) at >= 5 sigma; OR bottle tau_n drifting >= 5 sigma from 877.5-877.8 s at fixed gA

tfpt_fixed_point.statement: the E8 compiler content (D5+A3+mu4 carrier counting) has no slot for a new ~17 MeV boson with the required couplings -- like the sterile axis D, TFPT PREDICTS the dissolution of the ATOMKI anomaly family, not merely survives it.

fronts.reference: ATOMKI combined: MX = 16.85(4) MeV claimed in 8Be*, 4He*, 12C* internal-pair angular correlations
fronts.status_note: the originating anomalies persist at the source lab; interpreted as a protophobic ~17 MeV boson; no independent confirmation of the nuclear-transition excess

fronts.reference: MEG II, EPJ C 85 (2025) 763: 7Li(p,e+e-)8Be at 1080 keV, four weeks 2023
fronts.status_note: no significant signal; 90% CL limits R_17.6 < 1.8e-6, R_18.1 < 1.2e-5 (16.5-17.1 MeV) -- still compatible at ~1.5 sigma with the ATOMKI combination (Barducci+ 2025), so not yet a kill

fronts.reference: PADME Run III, JHEP 11 (2025) 007: resonant e+e- scan sqrt(s) = 16.4-17.4 MeV
fronts.status_note: blind analysis consistent with background over most of the range; largest deviation at 16.90 MeV with GLOBAL significance ~1.8-2.0 sigma (local 2.5). Run IV (2025/26, upgraded tracker + finer scan) underway -- the decisive dataset

axis_F_x17_dissolution.today: MEG II (2025) finds no signal in the same 8Be transition (though still ~1.5 sigma compatible with the ATOMKI combination); PADME's resonant scan is background-consistent except a 1.8-2.0 sigma GLOBAL excess at 16.90 MeV; PADME Run IV (upgraded, 2025/26) is the decisive dataset.
axis_F_x17_dissolution.kill_rule: a confirmed >= 5 sigma X17 resonance (systematics-controlled, replicated outside ATOMKI; e.g. PADME Run IV or MEG II full data)

tfpt_fixed_point.statement: TFPT reads the flavor sector as the frozen, exactly unitary CKM/PMNS assembly + SM gauge content; there is no compiler slot for the charged mediator (leptoquark/W') a real R_D(*) excess needs. Like axes D/E/F this is a DISSOLUTION prediction: SM values must return.

axis_G_rd_dissolution.today: HFLAV CKM25 combination sits at 3.8 sigma (3.5 with FLAG24 lattice SM) -- the most significant currently-standing dissolution target of the watchdog; Belle II full dataset and LHCb Run 3 decide this decade.
axis_G_rd_dissolution.kill_rule: R_D(*) excess confirmed at >= 5 sigma with independent tagging methods and consolidated SM form factors (lattice + dispersive agreement)

verdict_line: WATCHDOG: A=tension (3.7s pincer), B=consistent (lock excludes TFPT+real-w(z) by ~10^5.7), C=consistent (-0.43s Koide), D=consistent (sterile dissolution on track, MicroBooNE 2025), E=consistent (tau_n 877.5 s bottle-side -0.4s / beam -5.0s), F=consistent (X17 dissolving, PADME Run IV decides), G=tension (R_D(*) 3.8s standing -- sharpest dissolution target). No kill triggered (threshold 5.0 sigma).
